violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener ) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back of the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Non-food grade plastic containers observed being used for food preparation and storage. Food containers must be made of food grade materials. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
Non-food grade plastic containers observed being used for food preparation and storage. Food containers must be made of food grade materials. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerators and shelves have an accumulation of dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen cooking area was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

A bucket of cleaning supplies and used glove observed in the hand-washing sink in the prep area of the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Cut cabbage observed directly on unclean refrigerator shelf. Unwrapped lettuce observed stored directly on top of jars and containers of food in refrigerator. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was placed in 3 bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Boiled pineapple, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Cheese was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit in the small red refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cheese was relocated to another refrigerator. Food requiring refrigeration is not to be stored in small red refrigerator until it is operating properly. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Oxtail and chicken observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Breading mix for fish observed left out on the counter overnight. Breading mix must be disposed of after 4 hours once it has been used for raw animal products. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked pineapple was left overnight on the counter in container greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back of the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Trays from juice machine are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients.Scoops in other containers of dry ingredients are stored with the handles in the product. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Single-use items are being reused for food preparation and storage.Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen, and in the front service area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Pots and ingredient storage bin observed not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Non-food grade plastic containers observed being used for food storage.Food containers must be made of food grade materials. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the followinghave an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debrisrefrigeratorsfreezersshelvesstove topwaffle maker. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The hand sink in the prep area of the kitchen has an accumulation of soil residue and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The ceiling in the kitchen is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2026

(Oxtails and chicken ) was observed held at (50) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, food had been in 2 dr refrigerator for less than four hours. All food was removed from 2 dr refrigerator and placed in another unit. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

2 dr reach in located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Ambient temperatures was observed at 50df. Establishment not allowed to place any food products in unit until repaired and/or replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024
Handwashing sink in the kitchen observed in poor repair. Hot water knob on faucet does not dispense water. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The walls throughout the kitchen area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2024

The ceiling and ceiling vents in the kitchen area has an accumulation of dust and other debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The wall in the dining room area is in poor repair. Inspector observed a hole in wall behind wood structure The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
Handwashing sink in the kitchen is lacking signage. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (containers of dressings) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

Oxtails and chicken was observed held at (122-129) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC reheated food to proper temperature during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

Bags of leftover rice and shredded cheese was observed held at (46-51 ) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC disposed of rice and moved cheese to another unit(purchased today) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024

Cold hold units located in the kitchen area was not in good repair.2 dr reach in and 1 dr reach in were observed with ambient temperatures of 53 and 61DF.No food products are to be stored in these units until repaired/ replaced and properly functioning. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024
The handwashing sink in kitchen observed in poor repair. Hot water knob in faucet doesn't dispense water. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
The ceilings and ceiling vents in kitchen area has an accumulation of dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
The ceiling in the kitchen and steam table area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/05/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024
